zh.lifeSummary: Lucia Say是Ralf Roger de' Say的女兒，於大約1222年出生在英國史洛普爾。她於大約1243年與Hugh Meverel Lord of Gayton在英國英格蘭德比夏爾格拉頓結婚。他們至少是9個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1274年享年54歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lucia Say是Ralf Roger de' Say的女儿，于大约1222年出生在英国史洛普爾。她于大约1243年与Hugh Meverel Lord of Gayton在英国英格兰德比夏爾格拉頓结婚。他们至少是9个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1274年享年54岁。

nameMeanings.name: Say
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

variants.name: Sey
meanings: English and Scottish: probably a variant of   Say   particularly prevalent in Scotland.
Dutch and Flemish: from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal name Sigiheri (see Seger ).
West African (Ghana): Akan (Fante) name of unexplained etymology.

variants.name: Sayer
meanings: English: common occupational name from Middle English sayer, a phonetic variant of Sawyer , which in Wales and neighboring English counties may have been reinforced by the synonymous Welsh saer ‘carpenter’ and everywhere by a possible borrowing into Middle English of Old French seieor, saieur ‘sawyer’.

English: rare occupational name perhaps denoting an assayer of metals, or a servant who tasted his lord's food before serving, from Middle English saiour ‘assayer, tester’ (a shortened form of Old French assaiour, ultimately from Late Latin exagium, a derivative of exagmināre ‘to weigh’).
English: rare occupational name for a professional reciter or minstrel, from Middle English seier(e) ‘speaker’.

variants.name: Saye
meanings: Americanized form of Slovenian Saje: nickname for someone with very dark, sooty hair, derived from the root of sajast ‘sooty’, or directly from saja ‘soot’.
West African (Liberia): from the personal name Saye, found among the Mano (Mah) people, denoting a first-born boy.

nameMeanings.name: Lucia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine form of the old Roman given name Lucius, which is probably a derivative of Latin lux ‘light’. The girl's name is common in Italy and elsewhere, and is found as a learned, Latinate doublet of Lucy in England. St Lucia of Syracuse, who was martyred in 304 , was a very popular saint in the Middle Ages; she is often represented in medieval art as blinded and with her eyes on a platter, but the tradition that she had her eyes put out is probably based on nothing more than the association between light and eyes.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Lucia Say was born about 1222, in Shropshire, England as the daughter of Ralf Roger de' Say. She married Hugh Meverel Lord of Gayton about 1243, in Gratton, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 9 daughters. She died about 1274, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lucia Say est née environ 1222, à Shropshire, Angleterre, fille de Ralf Roger de' Say. Elle a épousé Hugh Meverel Lord of Gayton environ 1243, à Gratton, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 9 filles. Elle est décédée environ 1274, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lucia Say wurde um 1222 in Shropshire, England, als Tochter von Ralf Roger de' Say geboren. Sie hat um 1243 in Gratton, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom Hugh Meverel Lord of Gayton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 9 Töchter. Sie starb um 1274 im Alter von 54 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Lucia Say è nata il circa 1222 nel Shropshire, England come figlia di Ralf Roger de' Say. Lei sposato Hugh Meverel Lord of Gayton circa 1243, nel Gratton, Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 9 figlie. Morì circa 1274, all'età di 54 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lucia Sayは約1222年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアでRalf Roger de' Sayの娘として生まれた。約1243年にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアグラットオンでHugh Meverel Lord of Gaytonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1274年に54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lucia Say(은)는 대략 1222년 영국 슈럽실르에서 Ralf Roger de' Say의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1243년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 그래턴에서 Hugh Meverel Lord of Gayton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1274년 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lucia Say nasceu aproximadamente 1222, em Shropshire, Inglaterra filha de Ralf Roger de' Say. Ela casou-se com Hugh Meverel Lord of Gayton aproximadamente 1243, em Gratton, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhas. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1274, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Lucia Say родилась около 1222 г. в местности шропшир, Англия. Её отца звали Ralf Roger de' Say. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh Meverel Lord of Gayton около 1243 г. в местности граттон, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 9 дочерей. Она умерла около 1274 г. в возрасте 54 лет.
